title: Aruvikkuzhy Falls
tagline: Aruvikkuzhy Falls — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Aruvikkuzhy Falls (not to be confused with Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls) is a waterfall about 100 feet (30 m) high located about 2km from Thadiyoor and 7km from Kozhencherry in the Patha
overview.description: Aruvikkuzhy Falls (not to be confused with Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls) is a waterfall about 100 feet (30 m) high located about 2km from Thadiyoor and 7km from Kozhencherry in the Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. It is now becoming a popular tourist destination. The word Aruvikkuzhy means "a water stream with depth" in the Malayalam language (aruvi=water stream, kuzhy=deep hole).

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ayiroor, Pathanamthitta
topPlaces.description: Ayroor (also spelled Ayiroor) is a village near Thiruvalla, located in the western part of Ranni taluk, in Thiruvalla Revenue Division of the Pathanamthitta district in Kerala state, India. It is bordered by hills and located near the Western Ghats.

topPlaces.name: Thadiyoor
topPlaces.description: Thadiyoor is a town located in the Mallappally and Ranni taluks of the Pathanamthitta district. It is part of both Ezhumattoor and Ayroor Panchayaths and is located approximately 18 km from Thiruvalla and 11 km from Ranni.

topPlaces.name: Good Shepherd Kuriannoor
topPlaces.description: Good Shepherd School is an educational institute in Kuriannoor in Thiruvalla taluk, Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. It was founded by Professor V. A. Mathen Bose and Shanta Bose.

topPlaces.name: Melukara
topPlaces.description: Melukara is a small sub-village of Kozhencherry 689641 pinin Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. Its official languages are Malayalam, English and Hindi.

topPlaces.name: Keezhukara
topPlaces.description: Keezhukara, a small village in Pathanamthitta district, is about 135 km from Thiruvanandapuram, capital city of Kerala, India. It is on latitude 9.349 N and longitude 76.705 E. Pampa River is on the north and west.

topPlaces.name: Kalampala
topPlaces.description: Kalampala is a village in Ranni Taluk and Mallapally Taluk in the state of Kerala, India. It is 3 km (1.9 mi) from Ranni Town and 22 km (14 mi) from the Pathanamthitta district headquarters.

topPlaces.name: Kozhencherry
topPlaces.description: Kozhencherry is a census town in Pathanamthitta district of South Central Kerala in Kerala state, India. As of 2011 census, the population was 12,021 of which 5,594 are males while 6,427 are females.

topPlaces.name: Maramon Convention
topPlaces.description: The Maramon Convention, is the largest Christian convention in Asia, held at Maramon, Kozhencherry, Pathanamthitta, Kerala, India annually in February on the vast sand-bed of the Pampa River next to the Kozhencherry Bridge. It is organised by Mar Thoma Evangelistic Association, the missionary wing of the Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Church.

items.note: Ayroor (also spelled Ayiroor) is a village near Thiruvalla, located in the western part of Ranni taluk, in Thiruvalla Revenue Division of the Pathanamthitta district in Kerala state, India. It is bordered by hills and located near the Western Ghats.

Thadiyoor is a town located in the Mallappally and Ranni taluks of the Pathanamthitta district. It is part of both Ezhumattoor and Ayroor Panchayaths and is located approximately 18 km from Thiruvalla and 11 km from Ranni.
Good Shepherd School is an educational institute in Kuriannoor in Thiruvalla taluk, Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. It was founded by Professor V. A. Mathen Bose and Shanta Bose.
Melukara is a small sub-village of Kozhencherry 689641 pinin Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. Its official languages are Malayalam, English and Hindi.

Keezhukara, a small village in Pathanamthitta district, is about 135 km from Thiruvanandapuram, capital city of Kerala, India. It is on latitude 9.349 N and longitude 76.705 E. Pampa River is on the north and west.
Kalampala is a village in Ranni Taluk and Mallapally Taluk in the state of Kerala, India. It is 3 km (1.9 mi) from Ranni Town and 22 km (14 mi) from the Pathanamthitta district headquarters.
Kozhencherry is a census town in Pathanamthitta district of South Central Kerala in Kerala state, India. As of 2011 census, the population was 12,021 of which 5,594 are males while 6,427 are females.

The Maramon Convention, is the largest Christian convention in Asia, held at Maramon, Kozhencherry, Pathanamthitta, Kerala, India annually in February on the vast sand-bed of the Pampa River next to the Kozhencherry Bridge. It is organised by Mar Thoma Evangelistic Association, the missionary wing of the Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Church.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Aruvikkuzhy Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Aruvikkuzhy Falls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Aruvikkuzhy Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (93 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (81 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Aruvikkuzhy Falls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Aruvikkuzhy Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Aruvikkuzhy Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Aruvikkuzhy Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Aruvikkuzhy Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ayiroor, Pathanamthitta, Thadiyoor, Good Shepherd Kuriannoor, Melukara, Keezhukara.
